name: INR/USD Exchange Rate
summary: Day 69 Thursday. **INR ₹94.74 (-0.5% 1D) — RUPEE STRENGTHENED FURTHER ON BRENT COLLAPSE + DXY WEAKNESS; ₹95 TRIGGER REMAINS REVERSED.** Pullback continued from May 5 ₹95.36 record on (1) Brent -7% May 6 to ~$103, trading $101.95 May 7 morning — materially compresses oil-import CAD vector; (2) DXY 98.03 (4-year low) on Rieder Fed-chair speculation + Trump weak-dollar comments; (3) USD/KRW -1.5%, USD/JPY -0.9%, EUR/USD +0.3% — broader EM/G10 USD weakness regime; (4) RBI capital-flow curbs (NOP cap $100mn/day + INR NDF restriction) remain operational. FX reserves >$700B. ₹95.50 direct-intervention threshold UNTESTED. India 10Y G-Sec ~6.99% (-3-5bps from recent 7.05% high; about 50% crisis-linked, 50% RBI policy expectations). **The dollar-weakness regime is structurally bullish for INR if Rieder Fed-chair speculation hardens** — multi-quarter tailwind. Decision-relevant binary: if MOU signs, 5-15% IRR appreciation prices in within 7-14 days as sanctions-relief expectation builds → INR continued appreciation toward ₹93-94 range; if Iran rejects, INR weakens back toward ₹95-96 record territory. FII CY26 outflow already exceeds CY25 full-year ₹1.66T; FPI ownership ~16% (2-decade low, BELOW domestic).
